rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEGACY ENHANCEMENT POOLED SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/10/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The verification of the petition is incomplete. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (Notice of Remote Appearance, indicating that Counsel intends to appear remotely, is not the same as providing interested parties with instructions for how to appear remotely.) (3) Please file the Bloomingdale's receipts to verify the purchases of clothing, shoes, and accessories for the benefit of the beneficiary. (4) Please also file the receipts for purchases made via the Bank of America (desktop computer and airfare) and Synchrony Bank (bed) credit cards. (5) Slippers at Target cost $76.28? Please file the receipt. (6) Why are there so many purchases of "bedding/personal care items"? (Many purchases appear to be excessive and might also need to be supported by receipts, such as the electronics purchased at Target on "May-22" and personal care items purchased at Target on "Feb-23" and at The Container Store on "May-23", among others.) (7) There appears to be a duplicate payment of $76.28 to Target on "Sep-22" and another duplicate of $197.70 to Nike on "Sep-22". Please file receipts. (8) Please explain the market value changes and why, if the changes increased the account by $290.22, the "market value" is just $38,325.36 as of 3/31/23 when the "carry value" is $42,437.62. (9) The petition did not set forth the amount of legal fees and costs being requested. The amounts were only disclosed in the attorney fee declaration filed separately. After the other issues are addressed, an appearance may be required for the Court to determine if notice of the fees was sufficient. (This Court does not look favorably on multiple parties billing for internal conferences. Who is "GK" who also billed time?) (10) ORIGINAL bank statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/29/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPHINE MARIE MARKOVICH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Preliminary Distribution; For Partial Allowance Of Statutory Attorney'S And Administrator'S Compensation; Report Of Status Of Administration And Petition For Approval Thereof; And Request For Extension Of Time To Administer Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be address: Petition to state an approximate percentage of the ordinary services rendered by the fiduciary and her counsel up to this date. Petition seeks a partial payment (less than 75%) by merely stating the payments would benefit the estate to minimize income taxes; however, it is not clear how much of work of the administration have been completed. Prob. Code ec. 10830(c) authorizes the court to the compensation on account of services rendered. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD FREEDMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/10/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The only correct form of notice for initiating an estate is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Petition to Administer Estate, not a Notice of Hearing (with the fiduciary capacity completed when no fiduciary has been appointed yet). (2) The copy of page 10 of the will attached to the petitioner's declaration filed on 8/21/23 does not appear to be a true copy of the will; it is a copy of an unsigned document. (It is a petitioner's duty to provide an accurate, full copy of the will he seeks to admit, not the clerk's. If the original will the petitioner lodged was missing a portion of the will, he must disclose that.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/29/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARVA JEANNIE RIDGE-WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/4/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The waiver of bond by the other heir, Harold George Ridge, did not waive bond for the proposed administrator. A corrected waiver of bond must be filed. (In any event, a minimum bond of $20,000 will be required to protect California creditors.) (2) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/25/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GABRIEL JOAQUIN DOMENECH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/11/2023 (the earliest available date). The following procedural issues to be cleared two weeks prior: (1) The original will must be submitted. (2) Proof of publication (3) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (4) Proposed order. LRSF 14.7.A. (5) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number has not been filed. The form must be filed conventionally. LRSF 14.18.E and 14.59.C.7). (6) Petition #8 to identify the ancestor of petitioner, who is a sibling of the decedent. The date of death is needed. Also, petition to set forth if there is any other siblings (living or deceased) and their issues. All heirs (if any) must be noticed whether they are taking or not. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
